title: mentha longifolia var. subintegrifolia
intro: Mentha longifolia var. subintegrifolia, also known as Horse Mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of Horse Mint include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and bloating, as well as its ability to help reduce stress and promote relaxation. Horse Mint can be consumed as a tea, added to salads, or used as a flavoring agent in herbal preparations, making it a versatile and valuable plant in traditional medicine.
